DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF JOHANNA PACE'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR ADMISSION, SET ONE CLAIMANT'S MOTIONS TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, SET ONE, AND TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET TWO, NO.17.1, AS WELL AS FOR SANCTIONS, ARE DENIED. AT ISSUE ARE FOUR REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, AND FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 17.1, WHICH REQUIRES RESPONDENT TO STATE, WITH RESPECT TO EACH CORRESPONDING REQUEST FOR ADMISSION THAT WAS DENIED, THE FACTS, WITNESSES AND DOCUMENTS UPON WHICH THE DENIAL WAS BASED. OF THE FOUR REQUESTS, BOTH PARTIES APPEAR TO CONCEDE THAT THERE ARE DEFECTS IN REQUEST NO. 1 OBVIATING RESPONDENT'S NEED TO RESPOND THERETO. THE OVERLY BROAD REQUEST NO. 2 REQUESTS AN ADMISSION WITH RESPECT TO THE "SOLE CAUSE OF THE SUBJECT ACCIDENT". UNDER THE COMPLICATED AND YET TO BE FULLY ASCERTAINED CIRCUMSTANCES OF THE ACCIDENT, WHERE THE PRINCIPAL ACTORS HAVE INVOKED THEIR CONSTITUTIONAL RIGHTS, AND WHERE THE REQUESTED ADMISSIONS RELATE TO THIRD PARTY CONDUCT OUTSIDE OF RESPONDENT'S PRESENCE, RESPONDENT'S DENIAL OF REQUEST NO. 2 WAS PROPER, AS WAS ITS REFUSAL FOR SIMILAR REASONS TO ADMIT REQUEST NO. 3 AS TO WHETHER A VEHICLE IN QUESTION WAS "AN UNINSURED MOTOR VEHICLE". REQUEST NO. 4 WAS ALSO PROPERLY DENIED FOR THE REASONS CITED BY RESPONDENT. WITH RESPECT TO FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1, UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES, RESPONDENT HAS PROPERLY RESPONDED TO THAT, GIVEN THE ISSUES WITH RESPECT TO THE REQUESTS. SANCTIONS IN FAVOR OF CLAIMANT ARE NOT WARRANTED. PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED, COURT TO REVIEW AND SIGN AT A LATER TIME. JUDGE PRO TEM: ADRIAN COLLEY, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES (IN SUPPORT OF OPPOS TO PETITIONER'S MTN TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS AND FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 17.1) FILED BY DEFENDANT TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE)

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S MOTION TO COMPEL CLAIMANT'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND DOCUMENT PRODUCTION IS GRANTED, AS FOLLOWS. WITHIN 20 DAYS, MS. PACE SHALL SERVE VERIFIED FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AS FOLLOWS: 2.13, EXCEPT THAT SHE NEED NOT IDENTIFY BY NAME ANY PRESCRIPTION MEDICATIONS THAT ARE BOTH UNRELATED TO ANY INJURY SHE CLAIMS AND THAT DO NOT AFFECT ONE'S ABILITY TO PERCEIVE. IF THERE ARE SUCH PRESCRIPTION MEDICATIONS THAT FIT THIS CATEGORY, MS. PACE SHALL INDICATE SO IN THE RESPONSE TO THIS INTERROGATORY, WITHOUT IDENTIFYING THE NAME OF THE DRUG(S) OR THE PHYSICIAN. IF COUNSEL FOR MOVING PARTY SEEKS TO INQUIRE FURTHER, HE SHALL MEET AND CONFER WITH MS. PACE'S COUNSEL ON THE TERMS OF A PROTECTIVE ORDER TO OBTAIN THIS INFORMATION, AND THE PARTIES MAY SEEK FURTHER RELIEF IF NECESSARY; 6.7; AND 12.4. MS. PACE SHALL RESPOND FULLY AND COMPLETELY TO EACH SUB-PART OF EACH FORM INTERROGATORY IN SEQUENTIAL ORDER, WITH EACH RESPONSE IDENTIFIED BY THE SUBPART TO WHICH IT RESPONDS. MS. PACE SHALL ALSO SERVE VERIFIED FURTHER RESPONSES AND PRODUCE FOR INSPECTION AND COPYING RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION, NOS. 1, 2 AND 3, TO THE EXTENT SHAS NOT ALREADY DONE SO. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE ISSUED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: CAROL HARABURDA, CSR # 8052

Off Calendar Oct-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances on this matter on Tuesday, October 15, 2013. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with the understanding that the parties have reached an agreement with respective to the motion for protective order and the order of the court does not prevent or limit the parties from making an agreement pursuant to CCP 2024.060. The Court had issued the following tentative ruling re Plaintiff Johanna Pace's Motion For A Protective Order Precluding Further Depositions On The Basis That Discovery Is Closed: Motion for Protective Order Precluding Further Depositions is GRANTED in part. Depositions noticed for a date after October 4, 2013 shall not proceed. Any depositions noticed for October 3 or October 4, 2013 which were not completed due to the motion for protective order shall be rescheduled and completed prior to Arbitration. Moving Party's written objection pursuant to C.C.P. 2025.410 was served on Oct. 1, 2013, at the earliest, and could not apply to depositions noticed for dates within 3 days of that objection. Notice Of Motion And Mtion To Stay Arbitration
Off Calendar Jul-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: STEVEN CAMPORA, ESQ., 916.379.3500, FOR PLAINTIFF/CLAIMANT JOHANNA PACE.
JEFF FACKLER, ESQ., 925.933.7309, FOR DEFENDANT/RESPONDENT TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: RESPONDENT/DEFENDANT TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S Motion To Stay Arbitration IS Denied. Respondent has failed to show that this Court has jurisdiction to decide this motion, or even that there is any basis to do so at this time. This is not a discovery motion pursuant to CCP 2016.010, nor does Respondent identify any relief sought other than a stay. By contrast, there is a pending discovery motion set for hearing before the Court on July 22 regarding the testimony of Mr. Paez which could moot some or all of this motion. Further, the criminal trial of Mr. Mora is in less than a month. Respondent has not even stated when the arbitration is scheduled to begin. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admission, Set One
Off Calendar Jun-27-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nicholas Horton, Dreyer Babich Buccola Wood, 916.379.3500, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Jeff M. Fackler, Michel & Fackler, 925.933.7309, appearing for the Defendant.

Parties stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem. Matter argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court stated that it would adhere to the tentative ruling: Plaintiff JOHANNA PACE'S Motion to Compel Further Responses to Request for Admission, Set One Claimant's motions to compel further responses to requests for admission, set one, and to form interrogatories, set two, no.17.1, as well as for sanctions, are denied. At issue are four requests for admission, and form interrogatory no. 17.1, which requires respondent to state, with respect to each corresponding request for admission that was denied, the facts, witnesses and documents upon which the denial was based. Of the four requests, both parties appear to concede that there are defects in request no. 1 obviating respondent's need to respond thereto. The overly broad request no. 2 requests an admission with respect to the "sole cause of the subject accident". Under the complicated and yet to be fully ascertained circumstances of the accident, where the principal actors have invoked their constitutional rights, and where the requested admissions relate to third party conduct outside of respondent's presence, respondent's denial of request no. 2 was proper, as was its refusal for similar reasons to admit request no. 3 as to whether a vehicle in question was "an uninsured motor vehicle". Request no. 4 was also properly denied for the reasons cited by respondent. With respect to form interrogatory 17.1, under the circumstances, respondent has properly responded to that, given the issues with respect to the requests. Sanctions in favor of claimant are not warranted. Proposed order submitted, Court to review and sign at a later time. Judge Pro Tem: Adrian Colley, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Not Reported. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Toc Ompel Claimant'S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Document Production
Off Calendar Jun-21-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEVEN M. CAMPORA OF THE DREYER/BABICH & CAMPORA, TEL. NO. (916) 379-3500 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JEFF M. FACKLER OF MICHEL & FACKLER LAW FRIM, TEL. NO. (925) 933-7309 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
CAROL HARABURDA, TEL. NO. (925) 360-6065, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TRUCK INSURANCE EXCHANGE's Motion To compel Claimant'S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Document Production is Granted, as follows. Within 20 days, Ms. Pace shall serve verified further responses to Form Interrogatories as follows: 2.13, except that she need not identify by name any prescription medications that are both unrelated to any injury she claims and that do not affect one's ability to perceive. If there are such prescription medications that fit this category, Ms. Pace shall indicate so in the response to this interrogatory, without identifying the name of the drug(s) or the physician. If counsel for moving party seeks to inquire further, he shall meet and confer with Ms. Pace's counsel on the terms of a protective order to obtain this information, and the parties may seek further relief if necessary; 6.7; and 12.4. Ms. Pace shall respond fully and completely to each sub-part of each form interrogatory in sequential order, with each response identified by the subpart to which it responds. Ms. Pace shall also serve verified further responses and produce for inspection and copying responsive documents to Requests for Production, nos. 1, 2 and 3, to the extent shas not already done so. No monetary sanctions are issued. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Carol Haraburda, CSR # 8052 =(302/MJM)
